prevalence.source: IHME GBD / WHO + national survey context (DataSUS/mental health studies)
prevalence.trend: increasing post-COVID and with socioeconomic stressors

burden.mortalityRate: Indirect; elevated suicide risk and medical comorbidity

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: DSM-5/ICD-11: ≥2 weeks with depressed mood/anhedonia plus associated symptoms and functional impairment; exclude bipolar disorder and substance-induced mood disorder

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: variable; opportunistic screening in APS and CAPS referrals
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate; psychotherapy access limited, pharmacotherapy widely used

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: APS screening tools (PHQ-9) variably adopted; CAPS for moderate-severe and complex cases
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: SSRIs (sertraline/fluoxetine) and psychotherapy; crisis care and psychiatric referral for suicidality; integrated care in CAPS
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Stigma, limited psychotherapy availability, and uneven access to specialist services outside capitals
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: CAPS network and SUS availability of core antidepressants
